event: arrived at our Hospital’s Emergency Department
light pain in the right ear
redness of the skin behind the right ear
sought treatment from a general practitioner (GP)
sent to the nearest Ear-Nose-Throat (ENT) Service
HRCT right mastoiditis with partial sclerosis of mastoid air cells
treated with antibiotics Amoxicillin 500 mg three times a day (TDS)
visual acuity of 7/12 on her right eye
no signs of optic disc swelling on fundoscopy
left eye seemed to be fine
no clinical evidence of right optic nerve compression
MRI brain revealed non-opacification of the right cavernous sinus
lacunar image in right cavernous sinus on MRI
thickening of the nearby meninges on MRI
high-dose intravenous Ceftriaxone 2 g twice a day (BD) started
anticoagulation with Enoxaparin 8000 IU twice a day (BD) started
blood cultures positive for Streptococcus pneumoniae
chest X-ray revealed right lobar pneumonia
referred to the Intensive Care Unit (ICU)
antibiotics changed to Amikacin 20 mg/kg once a day (OD)
antibiotics changed to Piperacillin–Tazobactam 4.5 g three times a day (TDS)
piperacillin–tazobactam and amikacin planned for 10 days
hospital acquired pneumonia confirmed by bronchial aspirate culture
control contrast-enhanced magnetic resonance angiography (CE–MRA) performed
persistence of lacunar image in the right cavernous sinus on CE–MRA
continue oral anticoagulant for at least three months
oral anticoagulant will be stopped if resolution of the thromboses
